stories.title: Andy Burnham to promise to 'fix the big things' as incoming Prime Minister
stories.summary: Andy Burnham will be confirmed as Labour leader at a special party conference in London before taking over from Sir Keir Starmer as prime minister on Monday. He has promised to 'fix the big things' and has selected senior No 10 advisers.

stories.title: Keir Starmer makes Sadiq Khan a peer in the House of Lords
stories.summary: In one of his final acts as Prime Minister, Starmer has elevated London Mayor Sadiq Khan to the Lords, potentially opening the door for him to join the Burnham cabinet. 26 new peers were created in total.

stories.title: Starmer calls for Fifa investigation into Argentina over Falklands banner at World Cup
stories.summary: The outgoing Prime Minister has backed calls for Fifa to investigate Argentina players who displayed a banner asserting their country's claim to the Falkland Islands following their World Cup semi-final victory over England.

topics.name: Parliament
topics.description: House of Commons, House of Lords, PMQs & Legislation

stories.title: Business of the House debate outlines remaining stages of Representation of the People Bill
stories.summary: In the House of Commons, the Business of the House debate on 16 July outlined upcoming parliamentary schedule, including remaining stages of the Representation of the People Bill set for September.

stories.title: Reform UK scrutinised in Public Bill Committee over anti-vaccine conspiracy theories
stories.summary: Parliament's Public Bill Committee session on 16 July saw Reform UK's leadership come under scrutiny, with members raising concerns about the party platforming anti-vaccine conspiracy theories at conferences.

stories.title: Political Peerages July 2026 announced by government
stories.summary: The government published the full list of political peerages for July 2026, with nominations from the Labour, Liberal Democrat, and Conservative party leaders.

stories.title: Andy Burnham confirmed as Labour leader at special conference
stories.summary: Andy Burnham pledged to be 'unashamedly Labour' as he was confirmed as the new party leader, set to enter No 10 on Monday. He promised a 'new path' for the government.

stories.title: Burnham planning summer listening tour of UK in his first weeks as PM
stories.summary: The new prime minister-in-waiting is planning to travel across the UK throughout August while MPs break for the summer, framing it as a 'listening tour' to reconnect the government with voters.

stories.title: Greens accuse Burnham of being too influenced by 'vested interests' to offer real change
stories.summary: The Green Party's Zack Polanski pushed back against the narrative that Burnham would offer a more left-leaning agenda than Starmer, accusing the incoming PM of being captured by vested interests.

stories.title: Kemi Badenoch urges Burnham to back North Sea oil and gas drilling
stories.summary: Conservative leader Kemi Badenoch, speaking during a visit to Aberdeen and hosting an oil and gas industry roundtable, said the incoming PM should back increased drilling 'if he has any common sense'.

stories.title: Liz Truss assembles host of rightwing speakers for UK's CPAC conference in London
stories.summary: Former Prime Minister Liz Truss has assembled a roster of rightwing speakers including US influencer Jack Posobiec for the UK's CPAC event in London, as she seeks to gain influence among the British right.

stories.title: Five new Liberal Democrat peers appointed to House of Lords
stories.summary: Ed Davey welcomed five new Lib Dem members of the Lords: Julia Aglionby, Hannah Kitching, Tim Leunig, Dave McCobb, and Mark Valladares, bolstering the party's presence in the upper chamber.

stories.title: Counter-terrorism police take over investigation into Ann Widdecombe's murder
stories.summary: Former Conservative minister and Reform UK spokeswoman Ann Widdecombe was found dead at her Devon home on 9 July with serious injuries. Counter-terrorism police have now taken charge of the murder investigation.

stories.title: Man arrested for threatening to shoot Nigel Farage
stories.summary: Police have arrested a man who threatened to shoot Reform UK party leader Nigel Farage, amid growing concerns over politician security following the murder of Ann Widdecombe.

stories.title: Reform UK under scrutiny in Parliament over anti-vaccine conspiracy theories
stories.summary: MPs raised concerns in a Public Bill Committee session that Reform UK leader Nigel Farage has platformed prominent anti-vaccine conspiracy theorists at party conferences, calling for accountability.

stories.title: David Davis MP accuses Nicola Sturgeon of lying to cover up Murrell embezzlement
stories.summary: Senior Conservative MP David Davis claimed Nicola Sturgeon 'stitched up' Alex Salmond to cover up her husband Peter Murrell's fraud, alleging she lied about her knowledge of the embezzlement.

stories.title: What does Andy Burnham mean for Scottish Labour?
stories.summary: With Anas Sarwar having turned on Keir Starmer, analysis of whether Burnham's elevation as the 'King of the North' could be good or bad news for Scottish Labour's electoral fortunes.

stories.title: Scottish MPs and MSPs call on Danish shipyard to stop servicing Russian tankers
stories.summary: Scottish MPs and MSPs have joined over 100 parliamentarians from across Europe to demand a Danish shipyard cease servicing tankers linked to Russia, amid ongoing concerns over sanctions evasion.

stories.title: Starmer's final political peerages list published — 26 new Lords
stories.summary: The Cabinet Office published the full political peerages list for July 2026, including 16 Labour nominees, 5 Liberal Democrats, 3 Conservatives and 2 cross-benchers, including former Cabinet Secretary Sir Chris Wormald and retired judge Sir Brian Leveson.

stories.title: Government publishes update on Digital Gilt Instrument (DIGIT) pilot following Chancellor's Mansion House speech
stories.summary: Following the Chancellor's Mansion House speech on 14 July, the government published an update on steps to prepare for potential further issuance of the Digital Gilt Instrument, the UK's blockchain-based government bond pilot.

stories.title: Manor Ward By-Election: Labour wins in Stafford on 16 July
stories.summary: Labour's Aaron Thurstance was elected in the Manor Ward by-election on Thursday 16 July, defeating Reform UK and Conservative candidates. Reform came second with 414 votes, Conservatives third with 237.

stories.title: Old Catton Division Norfolk County Council by-election held on 16 July
stories.summary: A Norfolk County Council by-election took place in the Old Catton division on 16 July 2026, alongside a Norfolk Police and Crime Commissioner election.

stories.title: New voting intention poll shows Conservatives at highest sustained average since April 2025
stories.summary: A new Find Out Now voting intention poll (15 July) shows the Conservatives at their highest sustained voting average since April 2025, though Labour and Reform UK remain competitive.

stories.title: Sadiq Khan among 26 new peers given seats in the Lords by outgoing PM Starmer
stories.summary: London Mayor Sir Sadiq Khan has been elevated to the House of Lords in one of Keir Starmer's final acts as Prime Minister. The full list includes broadcaster June Sarpong, former union chief Christina McAnea, former Cabinet Secretary Sir Chris Wormald, and judge Sir Brian Leveson.

stories.title: Five new Liberal Democrat peers join the Lords
stories.summary: The Lib Dems gain five new Lords: Julia Aglionby, Hannah Kitching, Tim Leunig, Dave McCobb, and Mark Valladares, as part of the July 2026 political peerages list.

stories.title: Ann Widdecombe death prompts commentary on British political culture and Lords reform
stories.summary: The Guardian's Gaby Hinsliff wrote that Ann Widdecombe's killing should prompt Britain to ask what kind of political culture it wants, as the murder investigation enters counter-terrorism hands.
